hypothesis: If multi-member model ensembles are trained with a supervised loss plus an ensemble-consensus consistency loss that minimizes each model's distance to the ensemble mean on unlabeled data (with gradient detachment and coupling weight γ), then the semi-supervised ensemble will yield lower test error and improved calibration compared to purely supervised training and existing SSL baselines (mean-teacher, PSEUDOσ) across molecular graph and image benchmarks, with performance improving as ensemble size increases and remaining robust across choices of γ, label type, loss norm, and scheduling strategies.
